title: Flashpoint (planet)

{{Quote|Others say they find Flashpoint a wretched place, but I find it suits me. A world in a death-embrace with its sun, tumbling so quickly that day and night rocket by.|Entry #6109 in Demagol's journal|<ref name="SJDD:6005">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/fiction/demagol/index.html?page=1|text="The Secret Journal of Doctor Demagol"|int=The Secret Journal of Doctor Demagol (article)|newhost=Suvudu}}, Entry #6005</ref>}}
'''Flashpoint''' was an [[Atmosphere/Legends|airless]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] very close to its [[Flashpoint (star)|star]], with a day lasting just one [[Galactic Standard Calendar|standard hour]]. Being so close to its [[Star/Legends|star]] meant that the planet was bathed in stellar [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] and thus devoid of life. Before the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], a protective [[Magnetic field/Legends|magnetic field]] was set up and a [[Flashpoint Stellar Research Station|research station]] established by [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] to study stars. In [[3965 BBY]], this station was seized by [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|warriors]] as part of their [[Mandalorian Wars|invasion]] of [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. The Mandalorian scientist [[Demagol]] used the research station to experiment on [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in an attempt to discover the source of their [[Force power/Legends|powers]], but the Mandalorian presence was later expunged from Flashpoint by fugitive Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Zayne Carrick]] and his companions in [[3964 BBY]].

==Description==
Located in the [[Meerian sector/Legends|Meerian sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]],<ref name="TEAOC" /> Flashpoint was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] very close to its [[Flashpoint (star)|star]] and had no orbiting [[Moon/Legends|moons]].<ref name="KOTORCGpg" /> Flashpoint was a relatively new arrival in its [[Flashpoint system/Legends|star system]]<ref name="JJM-NewArrival">{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor08.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #8''}}</ref> and subsequently had a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] that was 1,776 local days long, with each day lasting only one [[Galactic Standard Calendar|standard hour]]. The proximity to its [[Star/Legends|star]] also meant that the surface of the planet—constantly bombarded by stellar [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]]—was extremely arid and lacked an [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] of any kind, leaving it void of life.<ref name="KOTORCGpg" />

A small area of Flashpoint was made habitable when a [[Magnetic field/Legends|magnetic field]] was set up on the surface by [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. The scientists also established a [[Flashpoint Stellar Research Station|research station]] on the planet to study stars.<ref name="CSWE" /> Surface travel was impossible outside this field due to the solar radiation clinging to the planet. The short nighttime had no effect on the radiation, and Flashpoint would cool only slightly during this time.<ref name="KOTORCGpg" />

==History==
[[File:Demagol-syringe.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Demagol carried out experiments on captive Jedi on Flashpoint.]]
{{Quote|This is Demagol, speaking into his personal recorder in the lab on Flashpoint. The Jedi subjects have taken control of the station!|Entry #6107 in Demagol's journal|<ref name="SJDD:6005" />}}
[[4000 BBY/Legends|Four-thousand years]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Galactic Republic established the Flashpoint Stellar Research Station on the planet to study the life cycle of stars,<ref name="CSWE" /> with funding for the station diverted from another [[Observatory (Metellos 3)|Observatory]] on [[Metellos 3]].<ref name="KOTOR37:PM2">[[Knights of the Old Republic 37|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 37]]</ref> In [[3965 BBY]], after the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], the nomadic [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] instigated the [[Mandalorian Wars]] by invading Republic worlds close to the edge of [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]]-controlled [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. One of these worlds was Flashpoint.<ref name="TEApg" /> [[3964 BBY|A year later]], [[Mandalore the Ultimate]], leader of the Mandalorians, awarded the captured Flashpoint research station to the Mandalorian scientist [[Demagol]]<ref name="SJDD:6005" /> as a location for him to carry out experiments on [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to discover the source of their [[Force power/Legends|powers]]. When the conflict between the Neo-Crusaders and the Republic escalated, greater numbers of Jedi were brought to Flashpoint,<ref name="KOTORH">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]''</ref> usually in small [[Teroch-type gunship|''Teroch''-type gunships]].<ref name="KOTORCGpg" />

Shortly after the [[Battle of Vanquo]] in [[3963 BBY]], fugitive Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Zayne Carrick]] and the crew of the [[578-R space transport|space transport]] [[The Last Resort (starship)|''Last Resort'']] traveled to Flashpoint to stage a rescue of [[Jarael]], a crew member who had been captured by Mandalorians on [[Vanquo]].<ref name="KOTOR8:F2" /> By posing as the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] of an apostatized Mandalorian [[Scout/Legends|scout]], [[Rohlan Dyre]], Carrick planted himself inside the research station, where he ambushed Demagol and took the scientist's [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]]. Dyre, together with a now-disguised Carrick, then distracted the Neo-Crusader sentries in the station's landing area while the Padawan used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to subtly place [[Mining charge/Legends|mining charges]] on nearby [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] projectors. [[Marn Hierogryph]], one of Carrick's other companions, detonated the charges remotely to leave only one Mandalorian craft, which the the remaining Neo-Crusaders used to flee. With their captors gone, the Jedi prisoners were able to leave Flashpoint while Jarael was reunited with Carrick and the crew. When they too departed, Flashpoint was left deserted.<ref name="KOTOR10:F3">[[Knights of the Old Republic 10|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 10]]</ref>

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|It was people of science who first settled here—and now, under my command, this station will do research again.|Entry #6019 in Demagol's journal|<ref name="SJDD:6005" />}}
The intense heat and radiation from its sun left the surface of Flashpoint entirely devoid of life.<ref name="KOTORCGpg" /> [[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]] [[Species/Legends|beings]], mainly scientific researchers, were able to reside on the planet after the Great Sith War as a result of the protection accorded by a magnetic shield.<ref name="CSWE" /> By the time of the Mandalorian occupation, the majority of Flashpoint's inhabitants were [[Human/Legends|Humans]],<ref name="KOTORCGpg" /> with sentries also residing in the research station to control the numerous Jedi prisoners being held for Demagol's notorious experiments.<ref name="KOTOR10:F3" />

==Locations==
[[File:Flashpointstation.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Flashpoint Stellar Research Station]]

===Flashpoint Stellar Research Station===
{{Main|Flashpoint Stellar Research Station}}
The only habitable location on Flashpoint was the Flashpoint Stellar Research Station set up by Republic scientists around 4000 BBY.<ref name="CSWE" /> Five projectors maintained a protective magnetic shield over the actual structure of the station as well as a landing area large enough to accommodate at least three [[Ministry-class orbital shuttle|''Ministry''-class shuttle]]-sized craft. The main part of the station was in an underground bunker and contained Demagol's experimentation room.<ref name="KOTOR10:F3" />

==Behind the scenes==
Flashpoint first appeared in [[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]], the second issue of the eponymous story arc of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Flashpoint|''Flashpoint'']]. The planet was further mentioned in several of the later issues, including a [[The Secret Journal of Doctor Demagol (article)|short story]] focusing on Demagol by [[John Jackson Miller]], the writer of the comic series. Flashpoint received entries in the [[2008]] ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. A precise location for the planet was given in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', published in [[2009]].

Miller has stated that he liked creating locations such as Flashpoint for the series, as it took readers to places they would not have seen before in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe.<ref name="Faraway-Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #36">{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor36.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #36''}}</ref>
